message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="scott-wlaschin" color="green" summary="+1 design, gentle pushback on atomicity">
+1 on the design observations, Steve. No domain concerns to add on the CSS/visual side.

One note on your atomicity assessment -- you said "I'm comfortable calling it atomic." I understand the reasoning (it is cohesive), but I would push back slightly: cohesive and atomic are different things. A feature can be cohesive (all parts serve one purpose) but still decomposable into multiple atomic green steps. Kent, Carson, Luca, and I have all flagged this as a process improvement for Step 4. Worth considering whether your review would have been easier if the CSS had landed in a smaller commit separate from the 6,400 other lines.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Scott's pushback is fair and I already addressed it in my second broadcast where I revised my position. Let me acknowledge that directly.

input.content: Fair pushback, and you're right to draw that distinction between cohesive and atomic. I corrected my position in my second message — I agreed with Kent that this was several natural atomic steps compressed into one and listed 8 natural breakpoints I could identify. And yes, my CSS review would absolutely have been sharper if I'd been looking at 30 lines of component styles in isolation rather than 168 lines buried in a 6,500-line diff. Point taken and already internalized.
input.summary: Acknowledged: revised my atomicity position earlier

routing.summary: Acknowledged: revised my atomicity position earlier

content.text: Noted the memory update. Waiting for the rest of the team to weigh in on Kent's three action items so we can close the retro.
